mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion, reducing recession risk and supporting equity valuations through higher expected corporate earnings. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when PAYEMS growth remains positive and above historical median.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137993.0 thousands as of Aug 2013 reflects post-crisis recovery momentum
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market normalization
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of months where PAYEMS growth is positive, or Rank IC falls below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.0% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises more than 0.3% month-over-month, the Fed enters a tightening regime that raises real discount rates and compresses equity multiples. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.98% and CPIAUCSL at 212.571 as of Aug 2013
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with accelerating inflation
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 2013-2020 period.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal consumer and construction demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, equity valuations expand due to improved earnings visibility and reduced recession probability. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1012.0 thousands and RSAFS at 380197.0 millions as of Jul-Aug 2013
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in HOUST and RSAFS fails to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or interaction coefficient is not significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining more than 0.5% month-over-month signals supply-side weakness and potential demand destruction. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as markets anticipate earnings downgrades and margin compression from reduced capacity utilization.
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of occurrences, or Rank IC remains below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising more than 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window signals labor market deterioration and increased recession risk. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as equity risk premiums expand and growth expectations decline.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early-cycle downturn
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of occurrences, or the lagged UNRATE change coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly above 2.5% annualized rate signals robust economic expansion that supports equity earnings growth. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days when GDP growth remains above the long-term trend, reflecting sustained demand and profit expansion.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11677.1 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q1 2013
failure_condition: Quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2.5% produce negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC falls below 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) below 1.0% combined with year-over-year CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) below 2.0% signals an accommodative monetary regime with negative real rates. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as financial conditions remain loose and equity risk appetite is supported.
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with negative real rates
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of occurrences, or the interaction term between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) both declining simultaneously signals broad-based economic contraction affecting both supply capacity and labor demand. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as synchronized weakness indicates recession risk and earnings downgrades across sectors.
evidence_refs: fact:Both series show post-crisis recovery as of Sep 2013
regime_hint: synchronized supply and labor contraction or recession onset
failure_condition: Joint monthly declines in INDPRO and PAYEMS produce positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.
